← Back to Home

Price Transparency
in Healthcare

Analyzing 6K rows by filtering out from 7.2 million raw hospital pricing records across three Louisville, Kentucky hospitals to expose negotiation gaps, payer disparities, and contract anomalies.

Python pandas Power BI DAX Star Schema CMS Price Transparency

Check out: Github

Background

Since 2021, the CMS Hospital Price Transparency Rule requires every U.S. hospital to publish machine-readable files disclosing their gross charges, cash prices, and payer-negotiated rates. In practice, these files arrive in incompatible formats — some wide with hundreds of payer columns, others long with one row per payer — making cross-hospital comparison nearly impossible without significant data engineering.

The original hospital pricing files contained 7,230,075 records combined for three hospitals. For this analysis, the data was narrowed to 49 high-impact healthcare procedures spanning inpatient, outpatient, emergency, laboratory, and pharmacy services, resulting in a curated dataset of approximately 6,681 records optimized for cross-hospital price comparisons.

CPT Procedures 48
Unique Payers 46
Max Price Spread $122K
Cash vs Min Rate $1.8K
Data Sources

Data was collected from publicly available CMS Hospital Price Transparency files. Each hospital publishes a different file structure, which was a challenge to clean and process these files to create a final clean dataset.

Challange: UofL's file used a wide format with one column per payer (379 total), requiring a melt operation to convert 97 rows into 1,940 long-format rows. Norton and Baptist used long format. I used LLM to format these files and consolidate into 13 columns for this analysis.

Check out code here: Github

University of Louisville Health
Louisville, KY
379 Columns
4,423 Output rows
40 Payer contracts
Baptist Health
Louisville, KY
28 Columns
420 Output rows
28 Payer contracts
Norton Hospital
Louisville, KY
30 Columns
1,838 Output rows
13 Payer contracts
Analysis

Business Questions:

  • Which hospital charges the highest gross charge for the same CPT code and how does their negotiated rate compare to competitors?
  • For which procedures does the negotiated rate exceed the gross charge and which payers and hospitals are involved?
  • Which payer consistently negotiates the lowest rates, and which plan type (Medicare Advantage, Commercial, Managed Medicaid) gets the deepest discount?
  • How does the cash price compare to the minimum negotiated rate are uninsured patients paying more than the best-insured patients?
  • Which procedures show the widest min-to-max negotiated rate spread, indicating the most volatile or inconsistent payer contracting?
  • How complete is each hospital's gross charge disclosure and does incomplete disclosure correlate with higher negotiated rates?
Data Model

Star Schema Design

Analysis: Key Findings
Cash Patients Pay $1,800 More Than Best Payer Rate
On average, the minimum payer-negotiated rate across all procedures is $1,800 lower than the cash price charged to uninsured patients. For the highest-cost procedures, this gap widens dramatically, uninsured patients face not the gross charge sticker price, but a cash price that still substantially exceeds what even the most expensive insurer pays.
$1,800 avg premium for uninsured

Price Transparency Gap

Trend Line: Black, Ratio Line: Purple

25 Most expensive procedures by hospital

Gross charge and Average Negotiated Rates overview

CPT 871: Negotiated Rate Exceeds Gross Charge
At Baptist Health, CPT 871 shows a gross charge of $61,621 with a negotiated rate of $23,961, sitting visibly above the diagonal reference line. This means the contracted payer rate exceeds the hospital's own listed price, representing a direct financial loss.
$61,621 → $23,961 above line
Norton Hospital:Minimal Payer Discounts
Norton Hospital's procedures (CPT codes 291, 292, 193, 310, 603, 872) cluster tightly along the diagonal reference line across the $10K–$40K gross charge range. Payers are achieving minimal discounts off Norton's list price.
CPT 470:74% Discount on Joint Replacement
CPT 470 (Major Joint Replacement - hip/knee) at Baptist Health shows the deepest payer discount in the dataset. With a gross charge of $122,570 and an average negotiated rate of $31,844, payers achieved approximately a 74% reduction from list price, the furthest dot below the diagonal.
74% below gross charge
18% Gross Charge Non-Disclosure at Norton & UofL
Baptist Health disclosed gross charges for 100% of procedure rows. Norton Hospital disclosed 83.24% and UofL Health 82.16%, leaving approximately 1 in 6 rows without a gross charge.
Baptist 100% · Norton 83% · UofL 82%
CPT 470 Skews All Hospital-Level Averages
Baptist Health's average negotiated rate of $15K is nearly 8× UofL's $1K average. However, this comparison is misleading — the hospitals bill for fundamentally different procedure mixes. CPT 470's $31,844 rate alone pulls Baptist's average dramatically upward.
Mean $7,740 · Median $1,284
Technical Detail

Key DAX Measures

Average Negotiated Rate
Avg Negotiated Rate = AVERAGE(Fact_Prices[negotiated_rate])
Median Negotiated Rate
Median Negotiated Rate = CALCULATE(MEDIAN(Fact_Prices[negotiated_rate]))
Gross Charge Coverage
Gross Charge Coverage = 
DIVIDE(
    CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(Fact_Prices), NOT ISBLANK(Fact_Prices[gross_charge])),
    COUNTROWS(Fact_Prices),
    0
)
Cash vs Min Negotiated
Cash vs Min Negotiated = AVERAGE(Fact_Prices[cash_price]) - AVERAGE(Fact_Prices[min_negotiated])
Discount % off Gross 
Discount % off Gross = 1 - DIVIDE([Avg Negotiated Rate],[Avg Gross Charge])
Price Spread
Price Spread = AVERAGE(Fact_Prices[max_negotiated]) - AVERAGE(Fact_Prices[min_negotiated])
Max Price Spread
Max Price Spread = MAX(Fact_Prices[max_negotiated]) - MIN(Fact_Prices[min_negotiated])
Scope

Procedures Included in Analysis

48 CPT codes were selected spanning emergency, surgical, imaging, laboratory, and inpatient services — chosen to enable meaningful cross-hospital comparison where the same standardized code appears at more than one hospital.

99285 · ED Visit 470 · Knee Replacement 29827 · Shoulder Arthroscopy 45378 · Colonoscopy 45385 · Colonoscopy w/ Polypectomy 43239 · Upper GI Endoscopy 29881 · Knee Meniscectomy 76830 · Pelvic Ultrasound 93306 · Echo w/ Doppler 93451 · Right Heart Cath 72148 · MRI Lumbar 73721 · MRI Knee 73221 · MRI Shoulder 74177 · CT Abdomen & Pelvis 74160 · CT Abdomen 72193 · CT Pelvis 871 · Complex Cardiac Procedure 872 · Cardiac Support 291 · Inpatient Psych 292 · Detox 310 · Medical Rehab 392 · Bone Marrow Transplant 690 · Kidney Procedure 641 · Misc Surgery 603 · Radiation Therapy + 23 additional CPT codes

Data sourced from CMS Hospital Price Transparency Rule (45 CFR Part 180) · Public domain hospital pricing files
